medical_history.chief_complaint: Eyelid twitches and blushing of the face
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Noticed eyelid twitches and blushing of the face in the first days of life. On admission, she was two months old, irritable and without eye contact. She had many daily seizures, manifesting as lateral eye deviation, blinking, chewing, drooling, and flushing, as well as 4-20 seizures with elevation of one arm, clonic jerks of the arm and leg, and cyanosis.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Second child after an uneventful pregnancy. Routine haematological, biochemical and blood gas analyses, as well as metabolic screening of urine, amino acids in serum, very long chain fatty acids in serum and organic acids in urine were normal. Ocular funduscopic findings and brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) were normal.

physical_examination.general_examination: Head circumference was 37.5 cm (45th percentile). Head lag was present and tone of the extremities was increased.

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Routine haematological, biochemical and blood gas analyses
Very long chain fatty acids in serum

other_tests.findings: Basic theta activity and multifocal spikes

Focal discharges from various areas of both hemispheres, showing sharp alpha and theta discharge over the right centro-parietal region, rhythmic beta discharge from the fronto-centro-parietal left region, and simultaneous involvement of two different areas with discharges of different frequencies
